_comment: OMA-URI settings for the 'RepoFabric - Peer caching' Custom Configuration Profile in Intune. Create one Custom profile (Windows 10+, Templates -> Custom) and add one row per entry below. See deploy/intune/README.md section 'BranchCache + BITS + DO endpoint configuration' for the step-by-step. Required for 0.8.0 PeerDist peer caching to work on managed Windows endpoints. Operated by RingoSystems Heavy Industries.

omaSettings.name: BranchCache: Enable
omaSettings.description: Turns on the BranchCache service on the endpoint. Required.

omaSettings.name: BranchCache: Distributed Cache mode
omaSettings.description: Sets BranchCache to peer-to-peer (Distributed) mode instead of Hosted (which would need a central cache server). 0.8.0 design relies on Distributed.

omaSettings.name: BranchCache: Cache age in days
omaSettings.description: How long cached blocks survive on the endpoint. Default in Windows is 28 days; we extend to 90 because installer files rotate more slowly than typical SMB content.

omaSettings.name: Delivery Optimization: Download Mode
omaSettings.description: Mode 1 = HTTP blended with peering behind the same NAT. Mode 2 = HTTP blended with peering across a private group. Mode 1 is the safe default; switch to 2 if your subnet spans multiple offices behind the same DO Group ID.

omaSettings.name: Delivery Optimization: Minimum file size for peering (MB)
omaSettings.description: Files smaller than this skip the peer-discovery dance. Default is 10 MB; we keep it at 10 so small installers (zip-only, snippet apps) bypass peer caching while large MSIs benefit.

bitsPeercaching._comment: BITS Peercaching is ADMX-backed (not pure OMA-URI). Use the Settings Catalog path: 'Administrative Templates -> Network -> Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) -> Allow BITS Peercaching = Enabled'. Without this, BITS will not participate in BranchCache and the entire PeerDist plan silently no-ops. See README for the Settings Catalog walkthrough.

firewall._comment: BranchCache needs two inbound Windows Defender Firewall rule groups: 'BranchCache - Content Retrieval (HTTP-In)' on TCP 80 and 'BranchCache - Peer Discovery (WSD-In)' on UDP 3702. Both are built-in groups in the firewall ruleset; enable them via the Endpoint Security -> Firewall profile in Intune, NOT via this Configuration Profile.
